div1.entryFree: Extract, to draw out: putting the hand in the pocket and --ing it clutched, Meas. III, 2, 50. compounded of many simples, --ed from many objects, As IV, 1, 17. a most --ing frenzy of mine own from my remembrance clearly banished his, Tw. V, 288 (i. e. drawing other thoughts from my mind. F2. Tw. V, 288 4 exacting; some M. Edd. distracting; cf. v. 287). that foreign hire could out of thee e. one spark of evil, H5 II, 2, 101. Partic. extraught == derived: knowing whence thou art extraught, H6C II, 2, 142.
